pattern: Because growth only begins once a seed finishes planting, and the planting order is entirely up to you, the seed with the longest growth ahead should be planted earliest so its growth runs alongside the planting of everything else.
hints: Since you choose the planting order, think about which seeds most urgently need their growth clock to start ticking.
Ordering seeds by how long they still need to grow, longest first, lets every long-grow seed's growth run in parallel with the planting time spent on the others.
Plant in that longest-grow-time-first order, keep a running total of planting days used so far, and the answer is the largest value of running-total-plus-that-seed's-grow-time you encounter.

edge_inputs.description: a small mix of plant and grow times with no ties
both plant and grow times at the maximum allowed value
all grow times tie, so the planting order among the tied seeds must not affect the final answer
the seed with by far the largest grow time also has a small plant time -- it must be planted first, not last, or the bloom day balloons
uniform plant and grow times across every seed

stress.description: random plant/grow times across the full allowed length and value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    plantTime = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    growTime = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [plantTime, growTime]
